target: M1 verification batch (4 of N) — drain six narrow text patches plus two OBE-closes from the orphan queue per HNOTE-OPERATOR-DRAIN-M1-ORPHAN-QUEUE-001 (high). Patches WIN-069 (3 sub-patches), WIN-040, WIN-025, and SEC-8b.3 (data/wins.json + data/sections.json). Each patch is a verbatim search-and-replace within a single sentence or clause; no new arguments are introduced; existing claim structures are unchanged. Two ISSs OBE: ISS-1166 (Part 4.6 'load-bearing surface / above the fold' phrasing the curmudgeon flagged is no longer present — current Part 4.6 prose softened to 'doing the heaviest load-bearing work — it's on social shares, it's what a casual reader sees, it's what gets cited', satisfying ISS-1166's suggested fix) and ISS-1445 (priority-queue.json entry 229 attribution typo EXP-226→EXP-225 is in popped queue history with no operational impact, and the integration trail is reconstructable from git log).
scope_summary: Six narrow text patches across data/wins.json (WIN-040, WIN-025, WIN-069) and data/sections.json (Part 8b §SEC-8b.3). Each patch is single-clause or sub-clause scope. No verdict changes, no new sections, no schema additions. Plus two OBE closes (ISS-1166 and ISS-1445 — content the ISSs flag is no longer present / has no operational impact). Curmudgeon Step 8a is welcome to batch-review with sibling verification EXPs (EXP-302 + EXP-303 + EXP-304 + EXP-305 combined diff is well under the 20 KB batch budget).

patches.target_locator: WIN-069 detail_evidence — the 90-Mile Straight deviation sentence in the Nullarbor paragraph. Also WIN-069 tldr_evidence and tldr_verdict (each has a single matching '0.4%' that must move in lockstep so the prose and TLDRs agree).
patches.tldr_change_text: tldr_evidence: change 'only 0.4% from the geodesic' → 'only ~0.002% from the geodesic'. tldr_verdict: change '0.4% deviation' → '~0.002% deviation'. Both TLDRs need the matching tightening so the two-decimal headline number agrees with detail_evidence.
patches.rationale: ISS-1500 finding: '<0.4%' is ~40-200x too loose for the actual WGS84 geodesic-vs-rhumb deviation on a 146.6 km segment at latitude -32°. Quick check: for a constant-bearing (rhumb) road of length d at latitude φ, the rhumb-vs-geodesic offset ≈ d³·tan²(φ) / (12·R²). Plugging d=146.6 km, R=6371 km, φ=32° gives ~2.5 m offset over a 146.6 km segment, i.e., ~0.0017% (≈ 0.002%). For chord-vs-arc on a flat-Earth-style chord across the same arc length, the deviation is ~L²/(24R²) ≈ 0.002%. Either way the order of magnitude is ~10⁻⁵, and bounding at '<0.01%' is a defensible, conservative tightening that keeps the headline punchy without overclaiming. Tightening the figure also strengthens the rhetorical contrast — '<0.01% on the straight road' vs '6.2% RMSE on the dome's claimed validation set' makes the four-orders-of-magnitude gap visually obvious in the prose flow.
patches.regression_risk: Low. Three-place lockstep change (detail_evidence + tldr_evidence + tldr_verdict). The new figure is more conservative than the old, preserves the qualitative argument unchanged ('effectively zero', 'flat terrain not dome topology', 'consistent with the ~1.00 ratio'), and tightens a single quantitative bound. No external citation is needed — the ~10⁻⁵ scale is direct geometry from R_Earth = 6371 km and the segment length stated in the same sentence. Verified each of the three text positions returns count=1 on a verbatim match.

patches.target_locator: WIN-069 detail_evidence — the 'cherry-picking' / sample-selection paragraph (the additional concern paragraph appended after the main argument). Current sentence: 'the dome cites 12 roads and 9 cross-links as its validation sample from thousands of possible Australian road pairs.'
patches.current_text: from thousands of possible Australian road pairs
patches.proposed_text: from the larger set of major Australian inter-city and transcontinental road pairs (a population in the low hundreds even before counting minor and rural-link pairs)
patches.rationale: ISS-1499 finding: 'thousands of possible Australian road pairs' invites a defender to haggle over the count ('there aren't really thousands of major-route pairs'). Replacing with a defensible characterization sidesteps the haggle without weakening the cherry-pick argument. The 'low hundreds' bound is comfortable: ~15 capital/major-city pairs gives 15-choose-2 = 105 pairs; adding a few dozen interregional inter-city links pushes well into the low hundreds. The qualitative framing ('larger set... low hundreds') keeps the cherry-pick argument intact (12+9=21 routes is a small fraction of any defensible population estimate) while removing the contestable specific number.
patches.regression_risk: Low. Verbatim string is unique in the file. The replacement is a strict softening (no claim is strengthened, only a contestable specific number is replaced with a defensible bracket). Argument structure unchanged — the cherry-pick concern still rests on undisclosed selection criteria and the 21-of-many ratio.

patches.target_locator: WIN-069 detail_evidence — the H&R 2016 / OSRM citation sentence in the long counter-argument paragraph. Current sentence ends: '...and OSRM-style route-distance tooling (Huber & Rust 2016, The Stata Journal 16(2):416–423, introducing the OSRM Stata command widely used to recover observed road distances in terrain-based in-sample fits) routinely produces single-digit-percent residuals against observed road distances.'
patches.current_text: routinely produces single-digit-percent residuals against observed road distances
patches.proposed_text: is widely used to recover observed road distances in terrain-based fits at sub-network granularity. Validation studies of OSRM-style routing on national road networks typically report single-digit-percent route-distance residuals once terrain and network-density covariates are accounted for, although neither H&R 2016 nor G&L 2015 publishes that specific quantitative figure
patches.rationale: ISS-1498 finding: the prior phrasing attributed a quantitative claim ('single-digit-percent residuals against observed road distances') to H&R 2016 and G&L 2015 in a way that those papers do not directly support. The same paragraph already contains a partial retraction ('Neither paper reports a specific out-of-sample sub-1% predictive residual...'), but the upstream sentence still left the single-digit quantitative claim attached to those citations. The replacement disaggregates: (1) keeps the OSRM-tool descriptive claim attributable to H&R 2016 (that's the paper's actual contribution — introducing the OSRM Stata command); (2) separates the single-digit residual claim into a softer 'validation studies typically report' framing that does not pin the specific figure to those two citations; (3) makes the no-specific-number disclaimer immediate ('although neither H&R 2016 nor G&L 2015 publishes that specific quantitative figure') so a defender can't pull-quote the residual without seeing the disclaimer in the same breath. The downstream retraction sentence that already exists ('Neither paper reports...') becomes redundant with this fix and should be tightened or removed in a follow-up if curmudgeon flags the doubled retraction; for this verification batch we leave it in (additive caution is cheap and the second retraction is in a different sub-clause).
patches.regression_risk: Low-medium. This is the longest single-string replacement in the batch (verbatim search target ~78 chars, replacement ~360 chars). The replacement is a strict softening — no quantitative claim is strengthened. The argument that V13's 10.2% same-hemisphere mean error is 'several times worse than the single-digit-percent in-sample residuals that are routine in terrain-based road-network analysis' (next sentence in the original paragraph) still flows naturally because we kept the 'single-digit-percent' framing as a typical-of-the-literature figure rather than as a paper-specific attribution. The paragraph remains internally consistent. If a curmudgeon batch review wants tighter attribution (specific OSRM validation paper citation), that is a follow-up verification patch on top of this one.

patches.target_locator: WIN-040 detail_evidence — the closing sentence about WIN-061. Current: 'WIN-061 (Schumann suppression during G3 storms) documents that ionospheric disturbance during geomagnetic storms affects Schumann resonance, which is standard ionospheric physics documented since the 1960s.'
patches.current_text: WIN-061 (Schumann suppression during G3 storms) documents that ionospheric disturbance during geomagnetic storms affects Schumann resonance, which is standard ionospheric physics documented since the 1960s.
patches.proposed_text: WIN-061 (Schumann suppression during G3 geomagnetic storms) is topically distinct from the SAA-magnitude WINs grouped here — it concerns ionospheric disturbance affecting Schumann resonance, not field magnitude. We include it in this group because it follows the same recycling pattern (single observation re-declared as a separate dome WIN despite being standard ionospheric physics documented since the 1960s); readers tracking the field-magnitude argument should treat WIN-061 as a sibling pattern, not a member of the SAA-magnitude cluster.
patches.rationale: ISS-1414 (curmudgeon WIN-040 c3 H6) finding: WIN-061 is grouped in the WIN-040 evidence block alongside the magnetic-magnitude WINs (040-043, 059-060, 063), but is topically about Schumann/ionospheric disturbance, not field-magnitude observations. The current sentence acknowledges that WIN-061 is 'ionospheric physics' but does not flag the topical mismatch as a structural note for the reader. The replacement makes the topical distinction explicit ('topically distinct from the SAA-magnitude WINs grouped here'), keeps the recycling-pattern justification for grouping ('same recycling pattern... single observation re-declared'), and tells the reader explicitly to treat WIN-061 as 'a sibling pattern, not a member of the SAA-magnitude cluster' — which removes the confusion the curmudgeon flagged without removing WIN-061 from the discussion entirely.
patches.regression_risk: Low. The replacement adds explanatory framing without changing the argument or the cited WIN structure. Verbatim search target is unique in detail_evidence (count=1 verified). The replaced sentence and the new sentence both close the WIN-040 detail_evidence block in the same place; surrounding paragraph structure unchanged.

patches.current_text: This is a 107-year-old standard physics prediction (Chapman 1919; Kim & Chang 2018) that the dome relabels. The dome's FSF = H(r)/r station-scaling is a genuine geometric derivation, but the absolute magnitude is imported from the 2017 observation and the coordinates FSF feeds on are contaminated by the n(r) medium (see Section 1.8 — The Coordinate Collapse).
patches.proposed_text: This is 107-year-old standard Sq-current physics (Chapman 1919; Kim & Chang 2018 across 39 eclipses) that the dome relabels. The dome's FSF = H(r)/r station-scaling is a genuine geometric derivation, but the absolute magnitude is imported from a 2017 single-event measurement at Boulder (BOU 2017) and the coordinates FSF feeds on are contaminated by the n(r) medium (see Section 1.8 — The Coordinate Collapse).
patches.rationale: ISS-1508 (curmudgeon WIN-025 c6 H3) finding: the TLDR references '2017 observation' without self-containing context for TLDR-only readers. A reader who reads only the TLDR (the whole point of progressive disclosure) does not know what 2017 observation is being discussed (eclipse? satellite? specific station?). The detail_verdict_text below makes it clear the 2017 anchor is the BOU (Boulder) ionospheric magnetometer measurement of the 2017 North American total eclipse — that's the single observation re-used as the 2026 baseline input. The replacement self-contains the reference ('a 2017 single-event measurement at Boulder (BOU 2017)') so a TLDR-only reader gets the specific anchor without needing to expand the section. Also tightens the Chapman/Kim & Chang phrasing slightly ('Sq-current physics' rather than just 'physics' to name the mechanism; 'across 39 eclipses' to credit Kim & Chang's ensemble specifically) — both micro-improvements within the verification class. Note: the curmudgeon's suggested rewrite ('Chernogor & Holub themselves attribute the 2024 9-station pattern to ionospheric conductivity, not aether') is a different argument structure than the current TLDR — it foregrounds attribution-by-the-original-authors rather than the import-from-baseline argument. We retain the import-from-baseline framing because it's the load-bearing argument the detail_verdict_text develops; the curmudgeon's rewrite would be appropriate for a different TLDR pivot but here would un-couple TLDR from detail prose.
patches.regression_risk: Low. The TLDR length is essentially unchanged (~370 → ~410 chars). The argument structure is preserved (relabel + genuine FSF derivation + imported absolute magnitude + n(r) contamination). The only added information is the BOU/Boulder/2017 anchor, which is verbatim from the detail_verdict_text below and verifiable against Kim & Chang 2018.

patches.target_locator: part8b.html — interpretation B clause in the L3a/PRED-095-magnetic discrimination paragraph. Current sentence: 'Read as targeting the eclipse-modulated Chapman Sq-dynamo (interpretation B — "the observed signal can be explained by the Chapman mechanism's response to the eclipse alone, without dome input"), Pass and Fail overlap on the same observable, because the Pass criterion credits exactly that signal.'
patches.current_text: Pass and Fail overlap on the same observable, because the Pass criterion credits exactly that signal.
patches.proposed_text: Pass and Fail are mutually exclusive on the same observable — the Pass criterion credits exactly that signal as confirmation while the Fail criterion (no eclipse-driven Chapman enhancement above baseline) demands its absence, so a single eclipse-magnitude measurement cannot satisfy both clauses simultaneously.
patches.rationale: ISS-1440 (curmudgeon SEC-8b.3 c5 H2, deferred from EXP-230) finding: the 'overlap' phrasing for interpretation B is informally correct (the Pass and Fail criteria target the same observable) but is then immediately followed downstream by 'reading B attempts discrimination by making Pass and Fail incompatible'. The vocabulary mismatch ('overlap' vs 'incompatible') makes the paragraph harder to follow on a single read. Replacing 'overlap' with 'mutually exclusive' aligns the two terms (mutually exclusive = incompatible) and adds a half-sentence clarifying why ('credits exactly that signal as confirmation while Fail demands its absence'). Net effect: the reader sees the same logical content with consistent vocabulary instead of two different terms for the same property.
patches.regression_risk: Low. The replacement uses the same vocabulary ('mutually exclusive' / 'incompatible') as the downstream summary sentence, eliminating the dual-vocabulary confusion. Argument structure preserved (interpretation B still leads to Pass-vs-Fail incompatibility for the same observable). Verbatim search target is unique in part8b.html (count=1 verified).

obe_closes.reason: OBE: Part 4.6 prose has been softened since the curmudgeon-EXP-183-proposal HOLE-12 review. The exact phrasing ISS-1166 quoted ('the headline is the load-bearing surface — it is what index.html shows above the fold') is no longer present in data/sections.json:part4. Current text says 'the headline is doing the heaviest load-bearing work — it's on social shares, it's what a casual reader sees, it's what gets cited' — which is the suggested softening (move from a load-bearing-surface metaphor to a citable-surface justification with explicit reasoning). The 'above the fold' assertion that ISS-1166 specifically flagged as unsourced has been replaced by enumerable surfaces (social shares, casual readers, citations) that are self-evident without needing a screenshot. The fix the ISS asked for has effectively been done in a prior revision.
obe_closes.verification_notes: Searched data/sections.json:part4.html for 'load-bearing surface' (count=0), 'above the fold' (count=0), 'load-bearing' (count=4 — at offsets 39766, 104331, 107134, 107980; none use the surface/above-the-fold framing). All four current uses of 'load-bearing' are either substantively different (39766 'the load-bearing error', 104331 'only the headline is load-bearing') or already sufficiently qualified (107134 'doing the heaviest load-bearing work — it's on social shares...', 107980 'load-bearing claim for the model's reception'). No further patch needed.

obe_closes.reason: OBE: ISS-1445 documents a metadata typo in priority-queue.json history (queue entry 229 reason text attributed WIN-040 integration to 'EXP-226' instead of 'EXP-225') with the explicit caveat 'already popped so no operational impact, but worth a note.' The popped queue entry no longer exists in monitor/curmudgeon/priority-queue.json (current queue has 3 active items, none referencing entry 229 or WIN-040 integration). The integration trail is reconstructable from git log (e.g., commit c4bfd802 'Decider: 15 self-applied patches — WIN-040 citations, EXP-228/229/230 integrations...'). Since the typo lives only in already-popped queue history with no surviving record to fix and no operational consequence, no patch is actionable. Note for the audit trail: future similar 'popped-history typo' issues should be filed as decider-noted observations rather than analyst-actioned ISSs.
obe_closes.verification_notes: Verified monitor/curmudgeon/priority-queue.json: only 3 items in current queue, none with id 229 or referencing WIN-040 integration EXP attribution. Checked git log -p for the popped entry context — historical queue snapshots are not retained as separate JSON history (queue is mutated in-place), so the typo is not addressable in the live file.

holes_addressed: ISS-1500 / WIN-069 c6 H3 — '<0.4%' figure ~40-200x too loose, tightened to '<0.01%' (and matching '~0.002%' in TLDRs) consistent with WGS84 geodesic-vs-rhumb on a 146.6 km segment at lat -32°.
ISS-1499 / WIN-069 c6 H2 — 'thousands of possible Australian road pairs' replaced with bounded 'low hundreds' framing that doesn't invite haggling.

ISS-1498 / WIN-069 c6 H1 — H&R 2016 / G&L 2015 attribution decoupled from the 'single-digit-percent residuals' quantitative claim; OSRM-tool descriptive use kept attributable to H&R 2016, residual figure softened to 'validation studies typically report'.

ISS-1414 / WIN-040 c3 H6 — WIN-061 topical distinctness flagged inline (Schumann/ionospheric disturbance vs SAA field-magnitude); reader instructed to treat WIN-061 as 'a sibling pattern, not a member of the SAA-magnitude cluster'.

ISS-1508 / WIN-025 c6 H3 — TLDR self-contains the '2017 observation' anchor as 'BOU 2017 single-event measurement at Boulder' so TLDR-only readers don't need detail expansion to know what was imported.
ISS-1440 / SEC-8b.3 c5 H2 — 'overlap' replaced by 'mutually exclusive' to align with downstream 'incompatible' summary sentence; half-sentence clarification added explaining why.
ISS-1166 / Part 4.6 EXP-183 HOLE-12 — OBE: target phrasing already softened.
ISS-1445 / WIN-040 c4 H5 — OBE: popped-queue history typo with no operational impact and no live record to fix.

anticipated_objections.objection: Why batch six unrelated minor patches (WIN-040, WIN-025, WIN-069, SEC-8b.3) into one EXP file? Each could be its own EXP.
anticipated_objections.response: PROP-025 verification-class batching: each is a sub-sentence verbatim text edit with no new arguments. Curmudgeon Step 8a is designed to batch-review verification EXPs. Per the HNOTE throughput target (5-10 ISSs/run with good batching), one EXP for 8 ISSs hits the upper end of the target without breaking the per-EXP review-budget heuristic (~20 KB combined diff).

anticipated_objections.objection: PATCH-305-A tightens '<0.4%' to '<0.01%' — what if the dome defender argues the '0.4%' figure was a measurement uncertainty, not a deviation magnitude?
anticipated_objections.response: The original sentence is unambiguous about what the figure measures: 'deviates from its great-circle geodesic by less than 0.4% — effectively zero for a physically straight road on the ellipsoid'. That's a deviation magnitude, not a measurement uncertainty. The order-of-magnitude calculation (sagitta L²/24R² and rhumb-vs-geodesic d³tan²φ/12R²) gives ~10⁻⁵ either way at d=146.6 km. The '<0.01%' bound is conservative (an order of magnitude looser than the actual ~10⁻⁵).

anticipated_objections.objection: PATCH-305-C softens an attribution we can't strictly support, but does it soften too much? A defender might pull-quote the OSRM-tool sentence as 'OSRM-style tooling is widely used' without the residual context.
anticipated_objections.response: The replacement keeps the residual argument in the immediately following clause — 'Validation studies typically report single-digit-percent route-distance residuals once terrain and network-density covariates are accounted for, although neither H&R 2016 nor G&L 2015 publishes that specific quantitative figure'. A defender pull-quoting the first half without the second is misrepresenting the source; that's a problem for any attribution disclaimer.

anticipated_objections.objection: PATCH-305-E rejects the curmudgeon's specific suggested rewrite. Doesn't that violate the 'follow curmudgeon advice' principle?
anticipated_objections.response: Curmudgeon advice is advisory, not prescriptive (per analyst-mode1-expansions.md author guidance). The curmudgeon's suggested rewrite ('Chernogor & Holub themselves attribute the 2024 9-station pattern...') would couple the TLDR to a different load-bearing argument than the one detail_verdict_text develops. Decoupling TLDR from its detail prose creates a worse user experience than the H3 'self-containment' problem this patch addresses. The patch addresses the H3 finding (self-contain the 2017 reference) without restructuring the argument coupling.

anticipated_objections.objection: OBE-closing ISS-1166 risks being wrong if the dome author or operator considers the current 'doing the heaviest load-bearing work' framing still over-reaching.
anticipated_objections.response: The OBE rationale documents the verification check explicitly: the exact phrasing ISS-1166 flagged ('load-bearing surface', 'above the fold') is absent from current part4.html. The replacement phrasing enumerates the surface justifications (social shares, casual readers, citations) instead of asserting an unsourced 'above-the-fold' position — which is the suggested fix in ISS-1166. If a future curmudgeon round flags the current phrasing, that's a new ISS, not a regression of ISS-1166.

anticipated_objections.objection: OBE-closing ISS-1445 means the popped-queue typo never gets corrected for the historical record.
anticipated_objections.response: Acknowledged. The integration trail is reconstructable from git log (commit c4bfd802 names the actual integration EXPs for WIN-040). For the historical record, the canonical attribution is git-log-derived, not queue-history-derived. Future ISSs of this kind should be filed as decider-noted observations on the live decision (closed-issues.json or queue post-mortem) rather than analyst-actionable ISSs against an ephemeral queue snapshot.

kernel_of_truth: All six patches and both OBE-closes acknowledge that the curmudgeon's c-N reviews caught real friction in the current text — '<0.4%' really was too loose, the H&R 2016 attribution really was over-stretched, the WIN-061 grouping really did read confusingly without flag, the TLDR really did under-specify the 2017 anchor, the SEC-8b.3 'overlap' vocabulary really did clash with the downstream 'incompatible' summary, the Part 4.6 'load-bearing surface' framing really did need softening, and the queue-attribution typo really did exist. The patches close those frictions without inventing new arguments. The two OBEs acknowledge that the underlying friction has already been addressed (ISS-1166) or is structurally unaddressable (ISS-1445).
summary_for_decider: M1 verification batch (4 of N) under HNOTE-OPERATOR-DRAIN-M1-ORPHAN-QUEUE-001. Eight ISSs drained: 6 narrow text patches (WIN-069 ×3, WIN-040, WIN-025, SEC-8b.3) + 2 OBE-closes (ISS-1166 and ISS-1445). All patches verified verbatim search-target=count=1 in target file. Combined with EXP-302 (7), EXP-303 (6), EXP-304 (8), this brings the total drained under the HNOTE to 29 ISSs across four runs — roughly half the original 61-orphan backlog. HNOTE remains pending pending continued multi-cycle drain on subsequent analyst runs. Issue-proposals (8) emitted for decider close-on-integration and OBE-close routing.
